title: muscari racemosum var. neglectum
intro: Muscari racemosum var. neglectum, also known as the autumn-flowering grape hyacinth, is a perennial plant species characterized by its clusters of small, bell-shaped flowers in shades of purple. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits associated with Muscari racemosum var. neglectum include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
